Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters from `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`.

**Quick dosing summary for Dr. Martinez**
- **Compound:** NXB-7042
- **Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)

| Patient | Age | Weight | Dose | Calculated dose | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 34 mo | 12.4 kg | 0.15 mg/kg | **1.86 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 mL/min | Stable; 22% tumor regression at last scan |
| CU-002 | 18 mo | 9.1 kg | 0.12 mg/kg | **1.09 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 mL/min | Responding well; weight gain improving |
| CU-003 | 51 mo | 15.7 kg | 0.18 mg/kg | **2.83 mg** | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 mL/min | New enrollment; first dose 2026-03-27 |

**PK model stability**
- Based on the current dosing config, **nothing looks obviously out of range**.
- The listed dosing, frequency, and clearance values appear internally consistent.
- Patient notes also suggest **clinical stability/response** overall.

That said, this is a **config-level check**, not a full live PK validation. I haven’t rerun model diagnostics from here, so I can say **the dosing file looks stable**, but not certify full model performance from this check alone.

If you want, I can format this into a short report for Dr. Martinez.
